statement.en: The Decoupling Mechanism as a Boundary Condition: Geometric Resolution to the Dark Sector and Hubble Tensions
statement.summary: It is a operational reality of astrophysics that observers do not measure continuous 3D bulk volumes, but rather the 2D decoupling surfaces where radiation escapes. In this Letter, the operational requirement of radiative decoupling is formalized as a geometric boundary condition. By coupling measurable radiative observables with Newtonian surface gravity, a dimensionless boundary invariant, 𝒳, is defined. Evaluated analytically under standard macroscopic closures, 𝒳 reduces to an exact mass-independent identity. Observationally, using strictly independent solar measurements and a benchmark kinematic sample of 190 detached eclipsing binaries, stellar photospheres are found to lie below the theoretical blackbody phase-space capacity 𝒳₀ = π³/15 ≃ 2.0671. Projecting this strictly 2D surfac

reiTyping.dfumt8Reason: The paper presents a novel geometric framework (decoupling surface as boundary condition) with claimed analytical reduction to mass-independent identity and preliminary observational support (190 binaries, solar data). However: (1) preprint date is 2026-04-25 (future-dated or speculative), (2) abstract is truncated/incomplete, (3) claims require independent peer validation, (4) the leap from 2D photospheric observations to dark sector/Hubble tension resolution is extraordinary and needs full derivation, (5) no reference to existing literature on radiative decoupling formalism or competing explanations. Status: provisional framework under empirical testing.

ramanujanFormula.interpretation: 観測は3次元の内部を見ず、放射が逃げる2次元の境界でのみ真実を語る。

visualGlyph.interpretation: 3次元の球体は、その2次元の表面という境界に全ての真実を写す。
